tldr: Move a Go cross-platform GUI to a declarative Slint UI when you want a modern UI markup, embedded targets, or a Rust/C++/Python backend.
why_switch: Declarative .slint UI language separates layout from logic
Bindings for Rust, C++, JavaScript, and Python broaden backend choices
Compiles to native with a tiny runtime suitable for embedded devices
Live preview speeds up UI iteration
Open-source dual license (GPL + commercial) per the Slint site
what_to_export: Widget tree and screen layouts from each Fyne app
Event handlers and the data they touch
App entry points and lifecycle hooks
export_method: Inventory each Fyne window: widget hierarchy, layouts, callbacks, and the Go data they bind to. Extract assets and theme values into a neutral location. Slint cannot consume Go directly, so plan to either rewrite the backend in a Slint-supported language (Rust, C++, JS, Python) or run Go as a separate process the new UI talks to via IPC. Verify language binding details in the official Slint docs.
import_method: Create a new Slint project following the official getting-started guide for your chosen backend language. Translate each Fyne screen into a .slint component with properties and callbacks. Wire callbacks to backend code via the language binding. Reuse exported assets and theme tokens. Iterate using Slint's live preview, then port logic screen by screen.

concept_mapping.note: Confirm exact layout names in current Slint docs
Updated from backend through generated bindings
No Go binding listed; treat Go as a separate service if kept
Follow Slint platform docs for embedded vs desktop

gotchas: Slint does not list a Go binding — plan for a backend rewrite or IPC
Dual licensing means closed-source distribution may require the commercial license; check current terms
Widget set is younger than Fyne's; some controls may need to be built
Mobile support differs — verify target platforms in the Slint docs
Animation and gesture handling differ; budget time for UX polish

email.subject: Proposal: migrate our Fyne app UI to Slint
email.body:
Team,

Proposing we move the desktop UI from Fyne (Go) to Slint. The win is a declarative .slint UI language, live preview, and tiny native runtime that even fits embedded targets. Bindings cover Rust, C++, JS, and Python.

The catch: Slint has no listed Go binding, so we either rewrite the backend in a supported language or split the app into a Slint UI plus a Go service over IPC. I'd start with one screen as a spike to validate the binding path and licensing fit.

Will share the spike plan and license review next.

Thanks
